_comment: Reference drift mismatch for Coinbase, derived from beliefs vs. public signals.
_status: Reference example the drift engine is refined on top of.
_framing: NEUTRAL: 'material change -> re-KYC needed', NOT an accusation. A human analyst decides.
_note_score: Risk now expressed as old/new risk_score (0-100); tier is the derived band. Coinbase intentionally does NOT flip band (stays MEDIUM 60->66) — the deliberate contrast with Meridian's LOW->HIGH flip. Drift = upward pressure within-band + a resolvable overhang.

alert.rationale: Onboarded as a US exchange/custodian with a contained product set (CB1) and footprint (CB4). Public record since shows material litigation (SEC suit, CB-EV4), product expansion into L2 infrastructure (CB-EV3) and derivatives (CB-EV6), and a widening EU regulatory perimeter (MiCA/BaFin, CB-EV5). The onboarding risk baseline understates the current profile.
alert.what_would_flip: Resolution of the SEC litigation and confirmation the new segments/jurisdictions are within licensed scope — note the SEC later dismissed its case (CB-EV7), which would re-baseline this downward.
alert.recommended_action: Refresh KYC scope; confirm licence coverage for derivatives & EU activity; re-baseline risk on litigation resolution.
alert._note: Coinbase is the SOURCING-breadth case: notice every evidence_id resolves to a real public citation. The drift is upward pressure + a resolvable overhang, not a LOW->HIGH flip.
